name: modal-dialog
role: Top-level scope for a modal surface. Renders as <div role='dialog' aria-modal='true'> (or role='alertdialog' for alert variant). Four variants — alert (destructive / important confirmation), form (input collection), panel (rich content / detail view), command (Cmd+K palette). Four sizes — sm (360px max), md (520px), lg (720px), full (mobile takeover). Manages focus trap, body scroll lock, inert background content, Escape + backdrop dismiss, focus restoration on close.

variants.alert: role='alertdialog' + aria-describedby pointing to the alert message. Used for destructive confirmations (Delete, Cancel subscription) and critical errors. Escape may be intentionally suppressed if the user MUST acknowledge — but use sparingly; most alert dialogs should still close on Escape.
variants.form: role='dialog'. Hosts <form> with form-controls. Used for 'Add subscription', 'Edit profile', 'Invite member' flows. Submit + cancel in footer.
variants.panel: role='dialog'. Hosts rich content — detail view, image preview, embedded video, settings sub-page. May not have a footer; close button in header is primary dismiss.
variants.command: role='dialog' + aria-haspopup='dialog' on trigger. Cmd+K palette — a centered modal with prominent search input + list of results. Special variant: no backdrop dim by default (transparent overlay so user can see context), or very subtle dim. Up/Down arrow keys navigate results.

sizes.sm: max-width 360px. Used for confirmation dialogs (alert variant primarily). Tight content; usually just a heading + 1-2 sentences + 2 buttons.
sizes.md: max-width 520px. The 80% choice. Used for most form dialogs.
sizes.lg: max-width 720px. Used for panel variant — rich detail views, multi-step flows, image previews.
sizes.full: max-width 100vw, max-height 100vh. Mobile-takeover — full-screen modal that replaces the page. Used at narrow widths or for immersive flows (onboarding, command palette on mobile).

lifecycle.opening: data-state='opening'. Fade-in transition over --motion-normal. Body scroll locked. Background content gets inert attribute. Focus moves to dialog's first focusable element (or the dialog itself if no focusables).
lifecycle.open: data-state='open'. Steady state. Focus is trapped — Tab cycles through dialog focusables; Shift+Tab cycles backward; Tab from last → first.
lifecycle.closing: data-state='closing'. Fade-out transition. After transition completes, dialog removed from DOM (or hidden); body scroll restored; inert removed; focus returned to trigger.
lifecycle.default: data-state='default'. Dialog not visible. Either not in DOM, or in DOM with [hidden] attribute and aria-hidden='true'.

ariaPatterns.ariaModal: aria-modal='true' tells AT this dialog is modal — siblings outside the dialog are inaccessible while open.
ariaPatterns.ariaLabelledby: Required. Points to the dialog's heading (usually inside modal-header).
ariaPatterns.ariaDescribedby: Optional. Points to descriptive text in modal-body. Especially important for alertdialog variant.
ariaPatterns.inertBackground: When open, set inert attribute (HTML spec) on every sibling of the dialog's root. This makes them non-interactive AND removes them from the accessibility tree. Polyfill for older browsers: aria-hidden='true' + tabindex='-1' on all focusable descendants.
ariaPatterns.bodyScrollLock: Set overflow: hidden on <body> (or <html>) while dialog is open. Restore on close.
ariaPatterns.focusTrap: On open: focus moves to first focusable inside dialog. Tab cycles through dialog focusables. Shift+Tab from first → last; Tab from last → first.
ariaPatterns.focusRestore: On close: focus returns to the element that triggered the dialog. Store the trigger in JS state.
ariaPatterns.escapeKey: Escape closes the dialog (for most variants). For alertdialog where dismissal is intentional, you may suppress this — but document the deviation.
ariaPatterns.backdropClick: Click on backdrop (outside dialog content) closes the dialog (for most variants). For alertdialog requiring explicit action, may suppress — but the close button in the header should still work.
ariaPatterns.asyncSubmitButton: When a form-variant dialog's submit triggers async work, the submit button gets aria-busy='true'. The cancel button gets aria-disabled='true' to prevent dismissal mid-submission.

name: modal-backdrop
role: Semi-opaque overlay behind the modal dialog. Click dismisses the dialog (except alertdialog variant where it may be suppressed). Renders as <div> with fixed positioning covering the viewport. Backdrop is conceptually paired with the dialog — they enter/exit together via opening/closing states.

dimLevels.none: Transparent. Used for command variant where the user needs to see context behind the palette.
dimLevels.subtle: 20% black. Used for command variant when slight dim is preferred.
dimLevels.default: 40% black. The canonical dim level for form and panel variants.
dimLevels.strong: 60% black. Used for alertdialog variant — strong focus on the dialog content.

ariaPatterns.ariaHidden: Always aria-hidden='true'. The backdrop is decorative; the dialog itself carries semantic role.
ariaPatterns.noTabStop: Not focusable. Click handler dismisses; doesn't participate in tab order.

name: modal-header
role: Top region of the dialog. Hosts the title heading (which the dialog's aria-labelledby points to) and an optional close button. Renders as a <header> inside the dialog.

meta.headingHierarchy: Default <h2>. The dialog is treated as a sub-section of the page; <h2> is the most common choice. Use <h1> only if the dialog is the entire page content (e.g., full-takeover variant on mobile).

ariaPatterns.headingId: The h2 id must be referenced by the parent dialog's aria-labelledby attribute.
ariaPatterns.closeButtonLabel: Close button uses aria-label='Close dialog' (or 'Close <dialog-title>' for more context). The ✕ glyph itself is aria-hidden.

name: modal-body
role: Content region of the dialog. Scrollable when content exceeds viewport / maxHeight. Hosts the dialog's primary content — form fields (form variant), descriptive text (alert variant), rich content (panel variant), or command-palette results list (command variant).

meta.scrollBehaviour: Vertical scroll appears when content height exceeds the dialog's max-height (default 85vh). The dialog's overflow-y: auto on this region keeps the header + footer pinned.

ariaPatterns.bodyId: If the dialog uses aria-describedby, this region's id is referenced.
ariaPatterns.scrollableRegion: If scrollable, may need tabindex='0' to make it focusable for keyboard users (allows arrow-key scrolling).

name: modal-footer
role: Bottom region of the dialog. Hosts the action buttons (Cancel, Confirm, custom). Renders as a <footer> with right-aligned button cluster by default. Composes <action-button> from pattern-button-system.

registers.primary-only: Single primary button (e.g., 'Got it', 'OK'). Used for informational dialogs.
registers.cancel-plus-primary: Cancel (ghost intent, leftmost) + Primary action (primary intent, rightmost). The 80% recommendation. Reading order: Cancel comes first; primary intent comes last (last position = highest emphasis).
registers.destructive: Cancel (ghost) + Destructive (critical intent). Used for delete confirmations. Same layout as cancel-plus-primary, but the rightmost is critical-intent, not primary.
registers.extended: Tertiary action (ghost, far-left) + Cancel (ghost) + Primary (primary). Used when there's a 'Save as draft' or 'Skip for now' option alongside Cancel + Submit.

ariaPatterns.buttonOrder: Cancel buttons live to the LEFT of primary buttons in the visual layout but should be the FIRST focusable element in tab order. This is intentional — keyboard users tab into the Cancel button first and must tab forward to reach the destructive primary action.
ariaPatterns.destructiveConfirmation: For destructive registers, the primary action's button text should name the consequence explicitly ('Delete subscription' not 'Confirm'). This guards against accidental destructive activation.

name: dialog-trigger
role: Element that opens a dialog. Not a primitive in its own right — it's a contract that any clickable element can implement. The contract: button (or link) carries aria-haspopup='dialog' and aria-controls='<dialog-id>'. On activation: dialog opens; trigger's aria-expanded becomes 'true'. On close: focus returns to the trigger.

meta.contract: Any button or link can be a dialog-trigger. Adds: type='button' aria-haspopup='dialog' aria-controls='<dialog-id>'. JS-side: clicking opens the dialog and stores 'this' as the focus-restoration target.

ariaPatterns.haspopupValue: aria-haspopup='dialog' (not 'true' or 'menu' or 'listbox'). The value specifies the popup type for AT.
ariaPatterns.controlsValue: aria-controls points to the dialog's root id. Required so AT can announce the connection.
ariaPatterns.ariaExpanded: aria-expanded reflects whether the dialog is currently open. Update on open/close.
